session_meta_insights: PARTIALLY_EXPLORED disjointness confirmed to produce lower QG outcomes than DISJOINT — 0 PASS verdicts, mean QG 5.87 vs 6.6-7.1 range for all prior DISJOINT sessions

Swanson_ABC_bridging is B-term-sensitive: add mandatory 'B-term AND Field C' PubMed check before assigning disjointness. B-term presence in Field C = PARTIALLY_EXPLORED regardless of cross-citation count
Zero kill rate in low-database-coverage domain is a warning signal, not a quality indicator — predicts low QG groundedness
Ranker-to-QG delta of 1.85 points (7.72 vs 5.87) is the largest in pipeline history — caused by testability/novelty inflation when KEGG/STRING returns no organism-specific data
In photosynthetic organisms under thermal/light stress, dominant ROS is singlet oxygen (1O2, k~10^7) not hydroxyl radical (OH, k~10^10) — 500x difference. Wrong ROS species = direct QG failure
Critic numerical corrections must be propagated into hypothesis text before QG — leaving errors as caveats results in QG penalty

Published gap extension bridge type (prior paper shows B-term in Field C under normal conditions, hypothesis extends to new stress context) produces 5.3-6.5 CONDITIONAL_PASS — lower than published unmeasured variable (8/10) or thermodynamic displacement (8.1/10)
Tool_repurposing T3 (volcanic glass dissolution x pharmaceutical ASD, scout 8.3, DISJOINT) is highest-priority unselected target in pipeline history — assign as Session 010 primary
Contradiction_mining T1 (Mn speciation paradox, scout 7.7, DISJOINT) is secondary priority deferred target
ABSOLUTE SELECTION RULE reinforced: when DISJOINT alternatives exist at comparable quality, never select PARTIALLY_EXPLORED target

Cross-model divergence pattern: Gemini endorses formal mathematical structure of hypotheses; GPT challenges biological premises. Divergence on H6 (Gemini 9/10 vs GPT 3/10) identifies the hypothesis that most needs empirical grounding of its parameter assumptions.
